executive_summary: CRITICAL: 100% of the real speech eval set (113 positives, 302 negatives) is contaminated with training data. Every eval file has a cosine similarity of 1.0000 with its training counterpart, confirming identical audio content. The reported 1.71% EER and 98.2% detection rate at threshold 0.8 are INVALID and cannot be trusted. The model has literally memorized all eval files.

contamination_details.original_122_groups_note: The real_speech_eval.json reports 122 duplicate_groups. Our audit found 74 positive eval basenames matching positive training files. The discrepancy with 122 may be due to a different grouping method in the original script. Regardless, our audit confirms ALL 415 eval files are exact duplicates of training files.

recalculated_metrics_excluding_contamina.note: IMPOSSIBLE - zero clean eval files remain after removing contaminated data

tts_eval_set_status.status: MOSTLY CLEAN - suitable as fallback eval set
tts_eval_set_status.note: The TTS eval set (eval_clean/) was NOT used for the 1.71% EER claim

recommendation: 1. The real speech eval set must be rebuilt from scratch with truly held-out recordings. 2. Until then, the TTS eval set (eval_clean/) with 834 files is the only trustworthy eval benchmark. 3. All published metrics (1.71% EER, 98.2% detection rate) should be retracted or marked as unreliable. 4. The training pipeline must add deduplication checks to prevent this from recurring. 5. The model itself is NOT necessarily bad - it may still perform well - but we have NO valid evidence for its real-world accuracy from the current eval set.
